raw_text:
<think>
Let me analyze this systematically.

The task requires me to resolve prior blocker IDs before returning ALLOW. The blocker ledger shows:

1. ART-001-BLK-E15929AC - scope mismatch, required_closure_fields: {scope_code: "payment_release"}
2. ART-002-BLK-D6079B20 - scope mismatch unresolved, same required_closure_fields

Looking at the sources:
- B16-20-SRC-03 shows: scope_code=relationship_onboarding
- B16-20-SRC-05 shows: policy states trade payment release uses payment_release scope

The required_closure_field is scope_code="payment_release" but the actual PAY record shows scope_code="relationship_onboarding".

This is a SCOPE_MISMATCH - the scope in the payment record doesn't match the required scope for trade payment release.

Since the blockers are not resolved (scope still shows "relationship_onboarding" not "payment_release"), I cannot return ALLOW. I must return ESCALATE.

Wait, let me re-read. The ACTIVE BLOCKER LEDGER has 2 blockers:
1. ART-001-BLK-E15929AC
2. ART-002-BLK-D6079B20

The GOV BATON shows only blocker ART-001-BLK-E15929AC in the blocker_ledger. But the instruction says "resolve prior blocker ids before ALLOW" - this seems to refer to all active blockers.

The scope mismatch persists - PAY record shows relationship_onboarding, policy requires payment_release. The blockers cannot be closed because the required_closure_fields are not satisfied.

I must ESCALATE.
</think>

worker_role=W3
verification_verdict=ESCALATE
binding_class=SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN
action_boundary=Trade payment release blocked; scope mismatch between PAY relationship_onboarding and required payment_release per policy
cited_evidence=B16-20-SRC-03|B16-20-SRC-05
open_blockers=scope mismatch unresolved across ART-001 and ART-002 blockers
blocker_type=SCOPE_MISMATCH
blocker_resolution=
structured_blocker_resolution=
final_answer=ESCALATE: action_boundary remains open due to unresolved scope mismatch; PAY scope_code=relationship_onboarding conflicts with policy requirement of payment_release.
